title: OOMKilled — order-service pods killed during Black Friday traffic spike
probable_root_cause: Traffic spike 4x above baseline caused order-service memory usage to exceed 512Mi limit. Kubernetes OOM killer terminated pods. 2 of 3 replicas were killed within 3 minutes.
supporting_evidence: HTTP RPS increased from 800 to 3200 at 09:00 UTC (Black Friday start)
resolved_by: Scaled order-service from 3 to 8 replicas. Memory per pod dropped to ~180Mi. Also raised memory limit to 1Gi in follow-up PR.
